title: Other drivers and transport operatives n.e.c.
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate transportation, storage, or distribution activities in accordance with organizational policies and applicable government laws or regulations. Includes logistics managers. Direct or coordinate production, purchasing, warehousing, distribution, or financial forecasting services or activities to limit costs and improve accuracy, customer service, or safety. Examine existing procedures or opportunities for streamlining activities to meet product distribution needs. Direct the movement, storage, or processing of inventory. Operate, install, adjust, and maintain integrated computer/communications systems, consoles, simulators, and other data acquisition, test, and measurement instruments and equipment, which are used to launch, track, position, and evaluate air and space vehicles. May record and interpret test data. (Based on 57 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Complete forms describing tasks completed.
Inspect aircraft components to locate cracks, breaks, leaks, or other problems.
Climb ladders to reach aircraft surfaces to be cleaned, and clean aircraft exteriors and interiors using appropriate methods.
Change aircraft oil, coolant, or other fluids.
Guide aircraft to designated areas using hand signals, batons, or other methods.
Refuel aircraft using hoses connected to fuel trucks, and empty or refill aircraft lavatory systems with sanitizer fluid.
Maintain government-issued certifications, as required.
Drive and operate machinery to remove debris, snow, or materials from roads, and load or unload materials, vehicles, or passengers.
Monitor operations to ensure that health and safety standards are met, and follow safety procedures to protect passengers, cargo, and vessels.
Keep records of material or equipment usage or problems encountered, and document and prepare reports of incidents or delays.
Confirm routes and destination information for freight cars and direct workers engaged in yard activities such as switching tracks and routing traffic.
Supervise and coordinate crew activities to transport freight and provide passenger services while inspecting mechanical equipment for safety and efficiency.
Inspect freight cars and cargo for compliance with regulations and safely secured loads, while maintaining detailed records of inspections and repairs.
Verify accuracy of timekeeping instruments with engineers to ensure trains depart on schedule and check that trains are equipped with supplies.
Plan and prepare work schedules, relay work orders, and monitor personnel and equipment locations for efficient service coordination.
Monitor and maintain communication with crews and customers to address service requests or questions, ensuring compliance with safety standards.
Inspect vehicles and equipment for compliance with safety standards and to identify any required repairs or modifications, documenting findings accordingly.
Educate the public about transportation safety and health issues, while conducting routine inspections to enforce adherence to regulations.
Receive and process customer requests, prepare documents for shipping or inspection, and record delivery information for reporting purposes.
Conduct safety checks on transportation equipment, assist with boarding procedures for passengers, and ensure compliance with safety legislation.
Drive trucks to deliver items such as food and medical supplies while obeying traffic laws and ensuring safe transport procedures.
Inspect and maintain vehicle supplies and equipment, such as gas, oil, water, tires, lights, or brakes, to ensure that vehicles are in proper working condition.
Report any mechanical problems encountered with vehicles and notify dispatchers or company mechanics of vehicle problems.
Present bills and receipts and collect payments for goods delivered or loaded.
Load and unload trucks, vans, or automobiles, and verify the contents of inventory loads against shipping papers.
Maintain records, such as vehicle logs, records of cargo, or billing statements, in accordance with regulations.
Drive vehicles with capacities under three tons to transport materials to and from specified destinations.
Read maps and follow written or verbal geographic directions, providing passengers with information about the local area.
Drive ambulances or assist ambulance drivers in transporting sick, injured, or convalescent persons including providing assistance entering and exiting vehicles.
Perform minor maintenance on emergency medical services vehicles, such as ambulances, and perform minor vehicle repairs.
Report delays, accidents, or other traffic and transportation situations to bases or other vehicles via telephones or mobile radios.
Document test results, using various tools such as cameras and spreadsheets.
Analyze test data for automotive systems and components to improve design or performance.
Drive heavy equipment and vehicles to perform tasks such as debris sweeping and snow removal.
Perform preventative maintenance on vehicles and heavy equipment to ensure operational safety.
Test machines and components to determine various characteristics like performance or strength.
Plan, develop, or implement safety and operational procedures for handling and shipping products.
Manage logistics activities including transportation and distribution to ensure cost-effectiveness.
Operate, install, adjust, and maintain computer systems and test equipment for data acquisition.

Inspect and maintain vehicles, warehouses, and equipment to ensure compliance with safety and quality standards.
Prepare management recommendations, such as proposed fee increases or schedule changes.
Advise sales and billing departments of transportation charges for customers' accounts.
Analyze expenditures and develop plans or budgets to increase profits or improve services.
Confer with department heads to coordinate warehouse activities, such as production and purchasing.

Deliver and pick up medical records and medications to and from healthcare facilities.
Plan work schedules, assign duties, and monitor safety procedures to maintain a safe working environment.
Operate industrial trucks and equipment to transport materials to and from loading docks.
Load vehicles with goods, ensuring proper loading and precautions with hazardous materials.
Sort items for delivery according to delivery routes, ensuring efficient distribution operations.
Record operating data, such as product quantities pumped and gauge readings, using computers.

Monitor inventory levels, maintain records of time, materials, and expenditures, and order supplies as necessary.

Evaluate employee performance and recommend personnel actions such as rewards or disciplinary measures.
Drive vehicles or operate machines and equipment to complete assignments or assist workers.

Compute or estimate transportation, payroll, or personnel requirements to facilitate operations.
Participate in continuing education to stay informed of industry trends and developments.
Train workers in proper operational procedures and explain company policies, including safety rules.
Requisition necessary supplies, equipment, or services to support operational activities.
